codeReview.summary: The board-display-polish feature cleanly extracts inline game.tsx grid markup into proper Board.tsx and Square.tsx components. All 9 expected behaviors (VAL-BD-001 through VAL-BD-009) are satisfied: 5x5 CSS grid with clear cell boundaries, readable non-truncated text, uniform square sizing, horizontal centering, native clickable button elements, visible New Game/Share placeholder buttons, page title set to 't3ingo — Playing', and hover state feedback. The implementation is well-structured with proper TypeScript interfaces, ARIA attributes (role='grid', aria-label), and responsive design (gap/padding/size adapt via sm: breakpoints). No blocking issues found.

issues.description: Hardcoded rgba() values for border and hover colors (e.g., rgba(50,143,151,0.2), rgba(79,184,178,0.12)) instead of CSS variables. The project defines a theming system with --line, --lagoon, --surface etc. that adapt to dark mode, but hardcoded rgba values will not. This is consistent with the pre-existing pattern from the original game.tsx inline buttons, so the worker followed existing convention — but both should ideally use CSS variables for dark mode support.

New Game and Share buttons are plain <button> elements with hand-crafted Tailwind classes rather than shadcn/ui Button components. AGENTS.md says 'use shadcn/ui components where appropriate' but no shadcn UI components are actually installed in the project (no src/components/ui/ directory). The hand-crafted approach works fine for placeholder buttons and matches the game's custom aesthetic, but the AGENTS.md guidance is misleading.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md says to 'use shadcn/ui components — use Button, Card, etc. from shadcn/ui where appropriate' but no shadcn/ui components are installed in the project. The components.json config file exists (new-york style, Tailwind v4), but src/components/ui/ directory is empty/nonexistent. Workers cannot follow this convention because the components aren't available to import. Either install the needed shadcn components or update AGENTS.md to reflect that hand-crafted Tailwind components are acceptable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md line: 'shadcn/ui components — use Button, Card, etc. from shadcn/ui where appropriate'; components.json exists at project root; LS of src/components/ shows no ui/ subdirectory — only Board.tsx, Square.tsx, Celebration.tsx, Celebration.test.tsx

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker reported that the frontend-worker skill's 'Write Tests First (Red)' step doesn't apply well to pure UI/visual features with no testable logic. This is a valid observation — the skill could benefit from a note clarifying when TDD can be skipped (e.g., for visual layout features where behavior is verified via browser automation rather than unit tests).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The frontend-worker skill mentions Write Tests First (Red) but for pure UI/visual components with no testable logic, this step doesn't apply well.' Worker followed procedure (followedProcedure: true) and noted this as a suggested change, not a deviation.

summary: PASS — The board-display-polish feature is well-implemented and fully satisfies all 9 validation assertions. Board.tsx provides a clean 5x5 CSS grid container with proper ARIA attributes, and Square.tsx renders individual cells as native <button> elements with min-height sizing (no truncation), hover feedback, and responsive design. The game page correctly sets the title to 't3ingo — Playing', and both New Game and Share placeholder buttons are visible. Two non-blocking issues noted: (1) hardcoded rgba colors instead of CSS variables, consistent with pre-existing pattern but won't adapt to dark mode; (2) plain buttons instead of shadcn/ui, which is actually the correct choice since no shadcn UI components are installed despite AGENTS.md suggesting their use. Two shared state observations: AGENTS.md's shadcn/ui guidance is misleading, and the frontend-worker skill could clarify when TDD doesn't apply.
